components.name: heat-inactivated human serum
components.role: complex base / carbon-nitrogen-lipid-protein source (undefined biological fluid)
components.evidence: Growth curves of P. gingivalis ATCC 33277 and Δpgn_1547 mutant growing in 20% heat-inactivated human serum supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin.

components.name: menadione (vitamin K3)
components.evidence: growing in 20% heat-inactivated human serum supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin

components.name: hemin
components.evidence: growing in 20% heat-inactivated human serum supplemented with 1 µg/mL menadione and 5 µg/mL hemin

components.name: anaerobic atmosphere (no O2)
components.evidence: P. gingivalis ATCC 33277 was cultured anaerobically at 37°C.

media.base_medium: 20% (v/v) heat-inactivated human serum (complex, undefined biological fluid) supplemented with menadione and hemin; the paper does not state the identity of the ~80% diluent/balance and cites no defined mineral base medium for this growth-curve assay.

sources_chased.what: Full-text methods (Bacterial growth conditions) and Fig. 3a caption for the serum growth-curve medium
sources_chased.how: corpus_path JATS XML full text (Read + grep)
added: explicit menadione 1 µg/mL and hemin 5 µg/mL concentrations
serum listed as explicit 20% (v/v) complex component

sources_chased.what: Any cited base-medium reference / trace-element mix / vitamin stock for the serum medium
sources_chased.how: grep for cited refs, serum, diluent, PBS, trace, vitamin, SL-10, Widdel; none present
added: none — the serum medium has no cited base recipe; it is serum + menadione + hemin only, and human serum is decomposed separately by the DB as a complex ingredient

media.notes: This is a complex biological assay medium, not a chemically defined mineral medium, so there is no hidden trace-element/vitamin stock to expand. The paper fully defines the additives (menadione 1 µg/mL, hemin 5 µg/mL) in both the Fig. 3a caption and the Bacterial growth conditions section; the ~80% balance/diluent of the 20% serum is not stated by the authors and was NOT fabricated. Serum is retained as a named complex component for separate DB decomposition. conc_mM computed from menadione MW 172.18 and hemin MW 651.94 (1 and 5 mg/L respectively). Note: routine TSB/BHI-blood plates (also hemin+menadione supplemented) are a separate maintenance medium and are not this record.
